---
title: "Week 1 — Facilitator Notes"
subtitle: "Command Line Basics"
---

## Learning Objectives

Residents should leave able to navigate the file system, create/move files, and understand paths. This is foundational for all subsequent weeks.

---

## Session Format

| Segment | Duration | Activity |
|---------|----------|----------|
| Demo | 15 min | Live navigation in terminal |
| Guided Practice | 30 min | Residents follow along with facilitator |
| Independent Exercise | 30 min | Residents complete file operations alone |
| Check-in | 15 min | Verify completion, troubleshoot |

---

## Key Teaching Points

### The Terminal is Just a File Explorer

Reframe: "The terminal does the same thing as clicking folders — just with text commands."

### Windows vs macOS/Linux

- **Windows users:** Emphasize Git Bash (not CMD, not PowerShell). Git Bash commands match macOS/Linux.
- **macOS/Linux users:** Terminal.app or any terminal works.

### Common Confusion Points

| Confusion | Clarification |
|-----------|---------------|
| "Where am I?" | `pwd` is your anchor. Check it constantly. |
| Spaces in names | Explain quoting or escaping early. Show failure first. |
| `cd` without arguments | On macOS/Linux, goes home. On Git Bash, also goes home. |
| `/` vs `\` | macOS/Linux use `/`. Windows uses `\` natively but Git Bash accepts `/`. |

---

## Live Demo Script

```bash
# Show where we are
pwd

# List files
ls

# Make a test directory
mkdir intro-demo
cd intro-demo
pwd  # Show the path changed

# Create files
touch file1.txt
touch file2.txt
ls

# Move one file
mv file1.txt subfolder/  # Error! subfolder doesn't exist

mkdir subfolder
mv file1.txt subfolder/
ls subfolder/

# Go up and back
cd ..
pwd
cd intro-demo/subfolder
pwd
```

---

## Exercise Solutions

**Part 1 Solution:**
```bash
pwd                              # /Users/resident (or similar)
ls                               # see home folder contents
cd ~                             # go to home (redundant if already there)
pwd                              # /Users/resident
```

**Part 2 Solution:**
```bash
mkdir mds-intro
cd mds-intro
touch notes.txt                  # Windows: type nul > notes.txt
ls                               # notes.txt
mv notes.txt week1-notes.txt
mkdir exercises
mv week1-notes.txt exercises/
cd exercises
ls                               # week1-notes.txt
```

---

## Troubleshooting During Session

| Problem | Quick Fix |
|---------|-----------|
| Can't open Git Bash | Right-click in folder → "Git Bash Here" |
| `touch` not found (Windows) | Use `type nul > filename.txt` instead |
| "Permission denied" on `rm` | Try without sudo first; if system file, abort |
| Lost in file system | `cd ~` to go home, start over |

---

## Assessment

Verify each resident can:

- [ ] Open terminal without facilitator assistance
- [ ] Navigate to any directory using `cd`
- [ ] Create a folder and a file
- [ ] Explain `cd ..` vs `cd ~`

If not, schedule 1-on-1 before Week 2.

---

## Preparation Checklist

- [ ] Tested demo commands on Windows and macOS
- [ ] Prepared troubleshooting handout
- [ ] Identified resident likely to need extra support
